sub_pixel_system.notes: No sub-pixel system documentation available for this sports-racing title

special_mechanics.name: Skiing/Downhill Movement
special_mechanics.description: Player races downhill in slalom skiing runs. Movement is momentum-based with speed maintenance by navigating around flags. Skiing on wrong side of flag triggers snowplow effect that slows the racer.

special_mechanics.name: Jump/Airborne Mechanics
special_mechanics.description: With sufficient momentum, players can jump over obstacles. Moguls (bumps) on terrain cause the racer to go airborne and slow down slightly upon landing. While airborne, players can perform freestyle tricks for bonus points.

special_mechanics.name: Freestyle Tricks
special_mechanics.description: Performed while airborne after hitting moguls. Successful tricks earn bonus points. Failed tricks result in tumble/fall and time loss.

special_mechanics.name: Collision/Obstacle Interaction
special_mechanics.description: Collision with trees, flags, snowmen, sledders, and other skiers causes tumble and time loss unless jumped over with sufficient momentum.

tier_notes: Tier 3 data: No precise physics parameters documented in available sources. Game is primarily described through gameplay mechanics and qualitative descriptions. Sources praise graphics, animations, perspective, and scrolling effects but contain no numerical physics values. Movement is fundamentally momentum-based skiing physics typical of 1980s arcade racing games.
